title: IVUS-Guided versus Angiography-Guided PCI in Unprotected Left Main Coronary Disease
authors: Jose Maria De la Torre Hernandez

title: Indicators of Global Climate Change 2025: annual update of key indicators of the state of the climate system and human influence
abstract: Abstract. In a rapidly changing climate, evidence-based decision-making benefits from up-to-date and timely information. We track twelve key sets of indicators of the state of the climate system, closely following Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) Sixth Assessment report (AR6) methods, to produce our fourth annual publication. One of the indicators, the Earth's energy imbalance (EEI) provides a crucial integrative measure of the overall heating of the planet and the pace of climate change – this has more than doubled since the 1976–1995 period. A newly added indicator of temperature extremes, the number of days experiencing marine heatwaves, has more than tripled between 1991 and 2025. For the 2016–2025 decade average, observed warming relative to 1850–1900 was 1.26 [1.13 to 1.36] °C, of which 1.24 [1.0 to 1.5] °C was human-induced. Human-induced warming reached 1.37 °C relative to 1850–1900 in the year 2025, increasing at a rate of 0.27 [0.2–0.4] °C per decade over 2016–2025. This high rate of warming, which matches the all-time high seen last year in the instrumental record, was caused by a combination of greenhouse gas emissions being at an all-time high of 54.6 ± 5.5 GtCO2e yr−1 over the last decade (2015–2024), as well as reductions in the strength of aerosol cooling. Despite this, there is evidence that CO2 emission growth is slowing. The continuation of these annual updates could track decreases or increases in the rate of human influence and climatic changes presented here, reflecting the outcomes of societal choices during the critical 2020s decade. The data presented herein can provide a useful reference point for the drafting of the IPCC seventh assessment report. In total, we employ analysis from over 40 global datasets (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20499280, Smith et al., 2026a). Future monitoring of these indicators, such as ocean and satellite measurements of the Earth's energy imbalance, are threatened by geopolitical and public funding decisions. Our ability to consistently track many of the indicators requires the continuity of observation programs and coordination mechanisms, including the Global Climate Observing System (GCOS) program, that enable their effective integration and use.

title: Who should introduce smart products in platform supply chains: manufacturer or platform?
abstract: Purpose Motivated by the observed real-world practices that either manufacturers or platforms are actively introducing smart products (SPs), this paper investigates a practically important question in platform-based supply chains: whether SPs should be introduced by manufacturers or by platforms? Design/methodology/approach We develop a Stackelberg game framework to investigate three potential strategies for introducing SPs (i.e. no SPs, SPs are introduced by the manufacturer and SPs are introduced by the platform) within a platform-based supply chain consisting of one manufacturer and one platform. The equilibrium decisions and profit outcomes under the three supply chain models are analytically derived and systematically compared. Findings Either the manufacturer or the platform may possess a cost advantage in introducing SPs. Regardless of the introducer, the platform always benefits from the introduction of SPs. In contrast, when SPs are introduced by the platform, the manufacturer may be worse off. As a result, a strategic conflict may arise, since both the manufacturer and the platform achieve higher profits when they introduce SPs themselves. Interestingly, we identify conditions under which the platform optimally acts as a free rider by allowing the manufacturer to introduce SPs, thereby eliminating the conflict. Originality/value Departing from the products innovation literature and traditional store-brand encroachment literature that focuses on manufacturers' traditional products (TPs) innovation or retailers/platforms' TPs encroachment, this study develops an analytical framework to compare alternative introduction strategies for SPs in platform supply chains. The analysis identifies the conditions under which manufacturers or platforms optimally introduce SPs, clarifies how consumer valuation would affect pricing decisions, demand allocation and welfare outcomes and provides managerial implications for firms operating in digitally enabled markets.

title: When mentoring works for women founders: an output function of accelerator mentoring
journal: Development and Learning in Organizations: An International Journal
abstract: Purpose Mentoring is the signature learning mechanism of accelerators, yet increasing mentoring volume does not reliably produce founder momentum. This paper resolves that dissociation by modeling mentoring as an organizational learning system that, in this sample, appeared most useful when it produced specific functional outputs, not on relational intensity alone. Design/methodology/approach Semi-structured interviews with 15 women founders in accelerator and innovation-center contexts were analyzed using inductive coding and cross-case comparison to construct a thematic map of how mentoring signals are interpreted and converted into action under uncertainty. Findings Effective mentoring produces three outputs: (i) navigation clarity (what to do next and who to call), (ii) credible access via sponsorship (introductions and advocacy that alter opportunity structure) and (iii) usable feedback that supports near-term experimentation while preserving founder confidence and action capacity. Two cross-cutting conditions constrained whether these outputs emerged: translation scaffolds that make guidance portable across context and identity-coded credibility dynamics, and boundary governance that protects legitimacy and psychological safety. When outputs were unreliable, founders did not simply disengage; they appeared to filter mentoring signals and tighten boundaries in ways that protected action capacity but slowed iteration and attenuated learning transfer. Practical implications Accelerators should manage mentoring as infrastructure: engineer early navigation sprints, specify sponsorship as a deliverable and track conversion, standardize feedback hygiene into short-cycle experiments, embed portability checks and enforce boundary rules with no-fault rematching to restore learning flow. Originality/value The study offers an output-specified mentoring architecture with preliminary indicators of where mentoring systems may break down, enabling accelerators to shift from counting encounters to designing routines that more consistently produce venture movement.

title: What keeps users coming back? The influence of experiential value on food delivery app continuous usage intention among Iraqi consumers
journal: International Journal of Quality and Service Sciences
abstract: Purpose Guided by the stimulus–organism–response theory, this study aims to examine the factors affecting consumers’ continuous usage intention of food delivery apps (FDAs) among Iraqi consumers. Particularly, it examines how experiential value dimensions, namely, efficiency, service excellence, visual appeal and gamification affect perceived value, which in turn predicts continuous usage intention. In addition, promotion is examined as a moderator on the link between perceived value and the continuous usage intention. Design/methodology/approach Data were collected via an online questionnaire survey using a judgmental sampling approach, resulting in 302 valid responses from individuals with experience using FDAs. Partial least squares structural equation modeling technique was used to test the proposed framework. Findings The findings reveal that the experiential value dimensions significantly enhance perceived value, which eventually leads to continuous usage intention. Data also supported the mediating effect of perceived value between efficiency, service excellence and gamification on continuance intention, except for visual appeal. However, the moderating effect of promotion on the perceived value–continuous usage intention relationship was not supported. Practical implications FDA providers should strategically invest in enhancing experiential value rather than relying solely on basic functionality. Emphasis should be given to building service excellence to strengthen long-term user ties. Furthermore, integrating gamification, enhancing efficiency and focusing on visual appeal should be treated as baseline requirements to enhance customers’ perceived value, leading to continuous usage of the FDA. The findings suggest that promotions used in isolation do not meaningfully deepen the link between perceived value and continued use. As such, FDA providers may consider integrating promotions with experience-based features (e.g. gamified discounts or rewards tied to app behaviors) to support long-term retention. Originality/value This study provides a newer perspective by highlighting experiential values as the key antecedents of perceived value in the context of FDAs. Unlike previous work that primarily focused on technical or functional aspects, this research emphasizes emotional and aesthetic aspects, namely, efficiency, service excellence, visual appeal and gamification. The findings contribute to the evolving understanding of how experiential elements influence digital service usage.

title: Unveiling the drivers of remote employees’ intentions to use home as a service consumption hub: a configurational approach
abstract: Purpose The purpose of this study is to investigate the factors influencing consumers’ attitudes and intentions toward in-home service consumption in the context of post-pandemic remote work. Drawing on the Push–Pull–Mooring framework, the authors bridge the services marketing literature with human resource and organizational behavior perspectives by adopting a configurational approach. Design/methodology/approach A survey of 245 UK-based remote workers was conducted. Data were analyzed using fuzzy-set qualitative comparative analysis to identify multiple equifinal configurations of work-from-home psychological effects, individual motivations and home quality perceptions that drive in-home service consumption. Findings Multiple alternative sufficient configurations reveal distinct combinations of hedonic, functional and convenience motivations, home quality, boundary control and psychological work-related factors, underscoring configurational trade-offs and substitution effects in shaping consumer attitudes and intention behavior. The findings of this study confirm complex interactions that extend beyond conventional linear, net-effects models. Practical implications This study highlights the growing importance of home as a consumption hub amid increased remote work. This paper offers insights for service providers to segment remote workers and tailor their service design, communications and offerings accordingly. Policymakers and marketers can better support well-being and sustainable consumption by addressing boundary management and loneliness. Originality/value This research extends the Push–Pull–Mooring framework to a residential, non-commercial consumption setting and integrates the psychological consequences of remote work with in-home service consumption using fuzzy-set qualitative comparative analysis’ configurational approach.

title: Türkiye’de Oyun Temelli Öğretim Üzerine Yapılmış Lisansüstü Tezlerin İncelenmesi
abstract: ÖzBu araştırmanın amacı, Türkiye’de oyun temelli öğretim üzerine yapılmış lisansüstü tezleri inceleyerek alandaki eğilimleri ortaya koymaktır. Çalışma kapsamında, Yükseköğretim Kurulu Ulusal Tez Merkezi’nde 2006–2025 yılları arasında yayımlanan 96 tez doküman analizi yöntemiyle değerlendirilmiştir. Veriler betimsel analiz tekniğiyle; tez türü, yıl, araştırma yöntemi, çalışma grubu ve incelenen değişkenler açısından analiz edilmiştir. Bulgular, tezlerin çoğunluğunun yüksek lisans düzeyinde yürütüldüğünü ve 2016 sonrası dönemde konuya yönelik akademik ilginin belirgin şekilde arttığını göstermektedir. Araştırmalarda en fazla nicel yöntemlerin tercih edildiği, karma yöntemlerin de önemli bir paya sahip olduğu; buna karşın nitel çalışmaların sınırlı kaldığı görülmüştür. Çalışmaların büyük ölçüde ilkokul ve ortaokul kademelerine odaklandığı, doktora tezlerinde ise okul öncesi dönemin ağırlık kazandığı belirlenmiştir. İncelenen tezlerde en sık ele alınan temalar “akademik başarı”, “beceri gelişimi” ve “öğrenme” olurken, motivasyon ve tasarım odaklı çalışmaların daha az yer aldığı tespit edilmiştir. Elde edilen sonuçlar, oyun temelli öğretimin eğitim alanında yaygın bir araştırma konusu olduğunu ortaya koymaktadır.Anahtar Kelimeler: Oyun temelli öğretim, akademik eğilimler, lisansüstü tezler, doküman analizi

title: The relationship between social media use, quality of life, and clinical symptoms in schizophrenia: a cross-sectional study
Does insecurity influence the financial inclusion-economic growth nexus in Africa? Insight from dynamic panel approach
journal: Journal of Economic and Administrative Sciences
abstract: Purpose Despite notable progress in promoting financial inclusion (FI) to drive economic growth (EG), Africa still lags behind other regions due to structural challenges, including inadequate infrastructure, regulatory barriers and low financial literacy, amongst others. More importantly, rising insecurity (INS) in the form of conflicts, terrorism, kidnapping, communal violence, etc., poses significant threats to the effectiveness of FI in fostering EG via disruption of financial systems, displacement of people, destruction of infrastructure and erosion of investor confidence. This study explores the influence of INS on the FI-EG nexus, particularly in Africa. Design/methodology/approach To assess the role of INS on the FI-EG nexus, this research employs the system-Generalized Method of Moments to analyse panel data for 39 African economies, further grouped into Lower Middle Income and Lower Income countries, during the 2004–2023 period. In addition, the study adopts the Principal Component Analysis (i.e. PCA) to construct the FI index. Findings The results portray that FI impacts EG positively (0.088/0.066), while rising INS erodes the growth-benefits of FI (−0.150/−0.184) in Africa in the full sample. This empirical evidence is consistent across different income groups. Moreover, agricultural output expansion promotes EG, but excess money supply impacts EG adversely. Research limitations/implications The study unravels the significance of ensuring greater security to reap the growth-benefits of FI in Africa. Originality/value The research is the first to explore the role of INS (proxied by military expenditure) on the FI-EG nexus in Africa.
